purpose: Detailed view of a single swarm agent/task. Merges static plan data (from initialization.json's agents array, passed via the `agent` prop) with live progress data (from progress/{task_id}.json, passed in `progressData[agent.id]`) using object spread so progress overrides plan. Renders id+title header, status/wave/layer/directory/assigned_agent badges, summary, dependency chips (resolved through findAgentFn callback to get sibling status colors), meta grid (started/completed/duration/status), files-changed list, live in_progress stage+message panel, milestones list, deviations list (with warning emoji label), and an activity log box that auto-scrolls to bottom on mount. Clicking a changed file dispatches GIT_NAVIGATE_TO_FILE through AppContext to jump into the Git Manager view, then closes the modal.
gotchas: Lifecycle data is merged with `{ ...agent, ...(agentProg || {}) }` — progress data wins on every conflict. If a plan field MUST be authoritative (e.g. wave, layer, directory) it must NOT also exist in the progress file or it will be overwritten silently.
Live elapsed-duration ticks every 1000ms via setInterval — ensure isRunning correctly transitions to false when completed_at appears, otherwise the interval leaks until unmount.
logsBoxRef autoscroll runs only ONCE on mount (empty deps array) — new log entries arriving via prop updates do NOT scroll to bottom. This is unlike LogsModal which has live autoscroll.
filesChanged path click dispatches an action to AppContext; the dispatch must be handled by GitManagerView's reducer or the click is silently ignored.

agent.id and agent.title come from initialization.json plan but merged.status/stage/message come from progress JSON — workers must write progress files in the dashboards/{id}/progress/ directory for live data to appear.
StatusBadge falls back to STATUS_COLORS.pending when status is unknown — invalid statuses render as 'pending' colored badges.
patterns: Inline StatusBadge sub-component is duplicated across AgentDetails.jsx and TaskDetails.jsx — they share visual style but are NOT extracted to a shared module.
Wrapper title is empty string ('') because this modal renders its own custom header layout (id + title block) instead of using Modal's default title bar.
Optional sections (deps, meta, files, progress, milestones, deviations, logs) are conditionally rendered with truthy guards — empty data shows nothing rather than empty placeholders.
conventions: Color values pulled from utils/constants STATUS_COLORS/STATUS_BG_COLORS rather than CSS variables to enable per-status alpha computation via colorWithAlpha.
splitFilePath uses lastIndexOf('/') — assumes POSIX-style paths in progress JSON, which is consistent with how workers report file changes.
